message.content:
You are verifier #2, independent. Read the ACTUAL code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and decide if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in current code (6 passes hardened). confirmed=true only if real, reproducible, worth fixing (not speculative/by-design).
J-003: Build backend (hatchling) fetched live and un-hashed during `pip install -e .`, defeating the hash-pin/reproducibility guarantee
File: .github/workflows/ci.yml:35
VERIFIED. All three jobs install with `pip install --no-deps -e .` (lines 35, 80, 191). `--no-deps` suppresses runtime deps but does NOT disable PEP 517 build isolation. pyproject.toml declares `build-system.requires = ['hatchling']` and uses hatchling.build (confirmed), and version is dynamic, so pip creates an isolated build env and fetches hatchling + its deps (editables, trove-classifiers) LIVE from PyPI, un-pinned and un-hashed, every CI run. Neither lockfile contains hatchling (`grep -ic '^hatchling==' requirements.lock requirements-core.lock` -> 0/0; 0 matches for any 'hatchling'). Consequences: (1) the workflow's central claim of installing 'EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned' is false for the build toolchain; (2) pip-audit (-r requirements.lock) never sees hatchling, so a compromised hatchling/editables patch executes arbitrary code during the editable build yet is never scanned — the exact #F-004 window the comments claim closed; (3) the SBOM omits the build-time dep set; (4) a yanked/changed hatchling makes a green commit non-reproducible.
PoC: grep -ic '^hatchling==' requirements.lock requirements-core.lock -> 0 and 0, while the package still builds via hatchling.build. On a clean runner `pip install --no-deps -e .` emits 'Installing build dependencies: hatchling, editables, ...' resolved live from PyPI with no --require-hashes and no lock entry.
Fix: Include the build backend in the hashed set and disable live build isolation in CI: (a) add hatchling to a build/dev group, regenerate locks with --generate-hashes, and install with `pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .` so the hashed hatchling is used; or (b) build a wheel in a separately-pinned hashed build env and pip-install that wheel. Extend SBOM/pip-audit coverage to the build toolchain.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this finding by examining the actual code.

content.content:
1	name: ci
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main]
6	  pull_request:
7	
8	# Least privilege (#H-008): the default GITHUB_TOKEN can be repo-write; this workflow only reads
9	# the code and uploads artifacts, so pin the token to read-only at the top level. Any job needing
10	# more must opt in explicitly with its own `permissions:`.
11	permissions:
12	  contents: read
13	
14	jobs:
15	  # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
16	  # (optional backends are import-guarded; integration tests skip). This guards ADR-0006.
17	  core:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    strategy:
20	      matrix:
21	        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
22	    steps:
23	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
24	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
25	        with:
26	          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
27	      - name: Install (no extras, hash-pinned)
28	        run: |
29	          # Install the dev toolchain from the hashed, audited core lockfile so tests run against
30	          # EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned and the SBOM describes — not a live PyPI
31	          # resolution where a malicious patch release could execute under test (#F-004). The
32	          # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
33	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
34	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
35	          pip install --no-deps -e .
36	      - name: Lint (ruff)
37	        run: ruff check src tests
38	      - name: Format check (ruff)
39	        run: ruff format --check src tests
40	      - name: Type-check (mypy)
41	        run: mypy
42	      - name: Test (pytest)
43	        run: pytest -q
44	
45	  # Extras lane: install the real backends and run the integration suite so the backend code
46	  # paths actually execute, and mypy type-checks against the REAL (not ignored) library types.
47	  # Service-dependent tests (ollama/qdrant/postgres) skip when the service is absent in CI; the
48	  # extra-only tests (langgraph graph, mlflow lifecycle, OTel, numpy primitives, methodology
49	  # e2e) run for real.
50	  integration:
51	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
52	    services:
53	      postgres:
54	        image: postgres:16
55	        env:
56	          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
57	          POSTGRES_DB: assay
58	        ports: ["5432:5432"]
59	        options: >-
60	          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U postgres"
61	          --health-interval 5s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 10
62	      qdrant:
63	        image: qdrant/qdrant:v1.12.4
64	        ports: ["6333:6333"]
65	    env:
66	      # loopback host keeps the data-sovereignty guard satisfied; creds are redacted on error
67	      ASSAY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/assay
68	    steps:
69	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
70	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
71	        with:
72	          python-version: "3.12"
73	      - name: Install (all extras, hash-pinned)
74	        run: |
75	          # Install from the hashed all-extras lockfile so the integration suite executes against
76	          # exactly the audited, pinned versions — closing the window where a compromised patch
77	          # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
78	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
79	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
80	          pip install --no-deps -e .
81	      - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
82	        run: mypy
83	      - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
84	        # #H-009: the live Postgres/Qdrant tests SKIP when the service is unreachable, so a
85	        # misconfigured/never-started container would let the integration job report GREEN without
86	        # actually exercising the backends. Fail loudly here if either service isn't up, so the
87	        # gate cannot pass vacuously.
88	        run: |
89	          set -euo pipefail
90	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
91	            pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -U postgres && break || sleep 2
92	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Postgres service never became ready"; exit 1; }
93	          done
94	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
95	            curl -fsS http://localhost:6333/readyz && break || sleep 2
96	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Qdrant service never became ready"; exit 1; }
97	          done
98	          echo "postgres + qdrant reachable"
99	      # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
100	      # numpy primitives, the T3 drain/classification (synthetic SDK, no metered call), AND —
101	      # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
102	      # Ollama and the metered frontier tier are not in CI and skip honestly.
103	      - name: Integration + unit tests with coverage
104	        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing
105	
106	  # Supply-chain: fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship
107	  # with advisories). The scan runs against the committed, fully-pinned, hashed lockfile
108	  # (requirements.lock) so the audit is reproducible across runs and matches what installs
109	  # (#108, #130, #131, #132, #145, #146). An allowlist file (.pip-audit-ignore, one advisory id
110	  # per line, '#' comments and blank lines allowed) covers accepted/unfixable ones.
111	  audit:
112	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
113	    steps:
114	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
115	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
116	        with:
117	          python-version: "3.12"
118	      - name: Install pip-audit
119	        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
120	        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
121	        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
122	        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
123	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
124	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
125	        run: |
126	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
127	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
128	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
129	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
130	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
131	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
132	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
133	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
134	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
135	          # stay in sync too.
136	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
137	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
138	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
139	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
140	        run: |
141	          set -euo pipefail
142	          # --strict: any dependency that cannot be collected/audited FAILS the gate (no silent
143	          # skips). --require-hashes: every pin must carry a hash. Build --ignore-vuln args as an
144	          # array so allowlist entries can't word-split or glob-expand, and read the file
145	          # newline-agnostically so the last line is honored even without a trailing newline.
146	          IGNORES=()
147	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
148	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
149	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
150	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
151	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
152	          fi
153	          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
154	      - name: SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile
155	        run: |
156	          set -euo pipefail
157	          # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
158	          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
159	          # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
160	          # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
161	          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
162	          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
163	          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
164	          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
165	          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
166	          IGNORES=()
167	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
168	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
169	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
170	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
171	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
172	          fi
173	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
174	          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
175	          echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
176	      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
177	        if: always()
178	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
179	        with:
180	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
181	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
182	          if-no-files-found: error
183	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
184	        run: |
185	          set -euo pipefail
186	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
187	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
188	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
189	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
190	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
191	          pip install --no-deps -e .
192	          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
193	          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
194	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
195	      - name: Upload license report
196	        if: always()
197	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
198	        with:
199	          name: license-report
200	          path: licenses.json
201	          if-no-files-found: warn
202	
203	  # Single required status check (#F-014): branch protection should require ONLY `all-checks`.
204	  # It fans in every other job, so adding a new job automatically gates merges without anyone
205	  # remembering to update the branch-protection rule — and `audit`/`integration` can never be
206	  # silently dropped from the merge gate by requiring just `core`.
207	  all-checks:
208	    if: always()
209	    needs: [core, integration, audit]
210	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
211	    steps:
212	      - name: Verify all required jobs succeeded
213	        run: |
214	          results="${{ needs.core.result }} ${{ needs.integration.result }} ${{ needs.audit.result }}"
215	          echo "job results: $results"
216	          for r in $results; do
217	            if [ "$r" != "success" ]; then
218	              echo "::error::a required job did not succeed ($r)"
219	              exit 1
220	            fi
221	          done
222	          echo "all required jobs succeeded"
223

1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["hatchling"]
3	build-backend = "hatchling.build"
4	
5	[project]
6	name = "assay-engine"
7	dynamic = ["version"]  # single-sourced from src/assay_engine/__init__.py:__version__ (#120)
8	description = "Dataset-agnostic engine for rigorous, reproducible empirical ML/NLP on security & privacy data."
9	readme = "README.md"
10	requires-python = ">=3.11,<3.15"
11	license = "Apache-2.0"
12	license-files = ["LICENSE"]
13	authors = [{ name = "thehipsterciso" }]
14	keywords = ["empirical", "reproducibility", "methodology", "security", "privacy", "ml", "nlp"]
15	classifiers = [
16	    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
17	    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
18	    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
19	    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
20	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
21	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
22	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
23	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
24	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14",
25	    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering",
26	    "Topic :: Security",
27	    "Typing :: Typed",
28	]
29	# The engine core is dependency-free; heavy backends are optional extras (lazy-imported,
30	# ADR-0006). The composed runner and methodology work with no extras installed.
31	dependencies = []
32	
33	[project.urls]
34	Homepage = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay"
35	Repository = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay"
36	Issues = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay/issues"
37	
38	[project.optional-dependencies]
39	dev = [
40	    "pytest>=8",
41	    "pytest-cov>=5",  # CI integration lane runs `pytest --cov` (#103)
42	    "ruff>=0.6",
43	    "mypy>=1.11",
44	]
45	# Heavy backends for the reasoning seam — installed only where real LLM calls run.
46	# The engine imports and unit-tests (mocking the backends) without these.
47	reasoning = [
48	    "langchain-ollama>=1.1,<2",
49	    "claude-agent-sdk>=0.2.101,<0.3",
50	    "anyio>=4,<5",
51	]
52	observability = [
53	    "opentelemetry-sdk>=1.25,<2",
54	    "opentelemetry-exporter-otlp>=1.25,<2",
55	    "arize-phoenix>=17,<18",  # single current major — a >=7 floor admitted 10 majors (#146)
56	    "openinference-instrumentation-langchain>=0.1.40,<1",
57	    "mlflow>=3.1,<4",
58	]
59	persistence = [
60	    "langgraph>=1.2,<2",  # core — provides MemorySaver (declared, not left transitive)
61	    "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres>=3.1,<4",
62	    "psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4",
63	    "qdrant-client>=1.9,<2",
64	]
65	orchestration = [
66	    "langgraph>=1.2,<2",
67	]
68	baseline = [
69	    "numpy>=1.26,<3",  # vectorized similarity at corpus scale (pure-Python fallback otherwise)
70	]
71	# Every backend in one shot (for a full local install). dev tooling stays separate.
72	all = [
73	    "assay-engine[reasoning,observability,persistence,orchestration,baseline]",
74	]
75	
76	[tool.hatch.version]
77	path = "src/assay_engine/__init__.py"
78	
79	[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
80	packages = ["src/assay_engine"]
81	
82	[tool.pytest.ini_options]
83	testpaths = ["tests"]
84	pythonpath = ["src"]
85	filterwarnings = [
86	    # The unkeyed-trail advisory (#F-049) is intentional and fires whenever run_study is called
87	    # without a secret — most tests deliberately use the unkeyed reproducible path. Suppress the
88	    # noise here; test_run_study_warns_when_trail_is_unkeyed asserts the warning fires explicitly.
89	    "ignore:run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED:UserWarning",
90	    # The no-recorder gate-node advisory (#F-007) likewise fires in many graph tests that drive a
91	    # gate node without a provenance recorder; test_make_gate_node_warns_when_no_recorder asserts
92	    # it explicitly.
93	    "ignore:make_gate_node:UserWarning",
94	    # The presence-only pre-registration advisory (#G-006) fires whenever a confirm primitive is
95	    # called with authority=None — most confirm unit tests do exactly that; one explicit test
96	    # (test_confirm_primitive_warns_on_presence_only_gate) asserts it fires.
97	    "ignore:confirm primitive called with authority=None:UserWarning",
98	]
99	
100	[tool.ruff]
101	line-length = 100
102	src = ["src", "tests"]
103	
104	[tool.mypy]
105	# No fixed python_version: mypy infers the target from the running interpreter, so the CI
106	# matrix (3.11–3.14) type-checks against each version rather than only 3.11 (#121).
107	packages = ["assay_engine"]
108	strict = true
109	
110	# Optional reasoning/observability backends are not installed in the type-check env; their
111	# imports are lazy and guarded at runtime, so treat them as untyped third-party modules.
112	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
113	module = [
114	    "langchain_ollama.*",
115	    "claude_agent_sdk.*",
116	    "anyio.*",
117	    "opentelemetry.*",
118	    "phoenix.*",
119	    "mlflow.*",
120	    "openinference.*",
121	    "langgraph.*",
122	    "qdrant_client.*",
123	    "psycopg.*",
124	    "psycopg_pool.*",
125	    "numpy.*",
126	]
127	ignore_missing_imports = true
128

content.text: Confirmed. Build system requires hatchling, version is dynamic. Let me verify the lockfiles don't contain hatchling.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && grep -ic 'hatchling\|editables\|trove-classifiers' requirements.lock requirements-core.lock 2>&1; echo "---no-build-isolation check---"; grep -c 'no-build-isolation' .github/workflows/ci.yml

content.text:
The finding is confirmed across all dimensions.

- All three `pip install ... -e .` invocations (lines 35, 80, 191) use `--no-deps` but NOT `--no-build-isolation`.
- `pyproject.toml` declares `build-system.requires = ["hatchling"]`, uses `build-backend = "hatchling.build"`, and version is `dynamic` (sourced via `[tool.hatch.version]`), so PEP 517 build isolation will fetch hatchling (plus editables/trove-classifiers/pathspec/packaging) live from PyPI on every editable install.
- Neither lockfile contains hatchling/editables/trove-classifiers (0 matches each). pip-audit and the SBOM run `-r requirements.lock`, so the build toolchain is never scanned.
- `--require-hashes` does not apply to the isolated build env; those fetches are un-hashed and un-pinned.

This is a real, reproducible gap between the workflow's stated guarantee ("EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned," "#F-004 window closed") and reality. The build backend executes arbitrary `setup`-equivalent code (hatchling build hooks) during the editable install under test, sourced live and unscanned.

Severity assessment: The comments explicitly claim the #F-004 arbitrary-code-execution window is closed; it remains open for the build toolchain, which runs code during install. However, the runtime/test dependency surface IS properly hash-pinned, so the exposure is narrower than the whole dependency tree (build-time deps of a single, well-known backend). It is a genuine supply-chain/reproducibility hole worth fixing but is one notch below a critical full-tree gap. I assess it as high.

input.reasoning:
Confirmed in current code. ci.yml installs the editable package with `pip install --no-deps -e .` at lines 35 (core), 80 (integration), and 191 (audit/license-gate). `--no-deps` suppresses runtime deps but does NOT disable PEP 517 build isolation, and none of the three add `--no-build-isolation` (grep: 0 matches). pyproject.toml line 1-3 declares build-system.requires=["hatchling"] / build-backend="hatchling.build", and project.version is `dynamic` resolved via [tool.hatch.version] (lines 7, 76-77). Therefore every editable install creates an isolated build env and fetches hatchling + its deps (editables, trove-classifiers, pathspec, packaging) live from PyPI, un-pinned and un-hashed, executing hatchling build code under the test/audit context. Neither lockfile carries the build toolchain: `grep -ic 'hatchling|editables|trove-classifiers' requirements.lock requirements-core.lock` returns 0 for both. pip-audit and the SBOM only scan `-r requirements.lock`, so the build-time dependency set is never audited and absent from the SBOM. This directly contradicts the workflow's own comments asserting it installs "EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned" and that the #F-004 arbitrary-code-execution window is closed — the window remains open for the build backend. Reproducible and worth fixing. The proposed fix (add hatchling+build deps to a hashed group, regenerate locks with --generate-hashes, install with --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e ., extend pip-audit/SBOM coverage) is correct.</reasoning>

content.text: Confirmed J-003 as a true high-severity production-readiness problem. The build backend (hatchling) is fetched live and un-hashed during every `pip install -e .` in all three CI jobs because `--no-build-isolation` is absent and the version is dynamic, defeating the hash-pin/reproducibility guarantee the workflow explicitly claims.
